issue.title: Fix plain issue number parsing
issue.body: Parsing issue references fails for `42` when running from a repository context.
issue.searchable_text: Fix plain issue number parsing parse_issue_ref src/github/client.py

comments.body: Please add a regression test for the number-only case.
comments.author: maintainer

retrieval_units.name: parse_issue_ref
retrieval_units.docstring: Parse issue references into owner, repo, and number.

retrieval_units.name: test_parse_issue_ref
retrieval_units.docstring: Regression coverage for issue reference parsing.

expected_response.core_problem: The parser handles URL and owner/repo references but misses the plain-number case when repo context is available.
strategic_guidance: Reproduce the number-only parsing path before editing the parser.
Trace how repo_hint is used when the ref is just a number.
Compare the plain-number branch to the existing URL and owner/repo#num branches.
Keep the accepted existing formats intact while adding the new case.
suggested_approach: Add a failing regression test for a plain issue number with repo context.
Update parse_issue_ref to convert a number-only ref into owner/repo/number when repo_hint exists.
Run the focused client tests and verify the other reference formats still pass.
positive_signals: The issue points to a specific parser entry point.
warning_signals: Parser changes can break other accepted issue reference formats.
why_these_files: src/github/client.py contains the parsing function named in the issue.
tests/test_client.py is the right place for the regression test requested by the maintainer.
